purpose: Paper-facing comparison of how the Ethan OpenFOAM CFD and current external Fluid 1D model treat internal Nusselt number, internal HTC, HX thermal closure, and external wall boundary modeling.
headline_findings: The OpenFOAM CFD does not impose an internal Nusselt-number closure; internal Nu/HTC is postprocessed from wall heat flux and wall-to-bulk temperature differences.
The current 1D Fluid model explicitly closes internal convection with a laminar fully-developed/developing Nusselt maximum below Re=2300 and a Dittus-Boelter-style branch above Re=2300.
The OpenFOAM outside-wall treatment is a prescribed external-temperature/layer-resistance boundary model, not a resolved coupled outside-air convective CFD domain.
Current CFD effective Nu/UA/HTC closure observations should remain diagnostic until support, sign, bulk-temperature, and mesh-UQ gates are satisfied.
The largest current temperature-agreement shift in fixed-mdot replay is tied to the cooler/HX boundary duty, so internal Nu should not be blamed in isolation.
